steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $17 \left(x-10y\right) \left(x+10y\right)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Recognize the difference of squares
steps.content: Inside the parentheses, $x^2-100y^2$ is a difference of squares because $100y^2=(10y)^2$. Use the pattern $a^2-b^2=(a-b)(a+b)$.
